access the AWS environment ... using the AWS Golang SDK

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained and used the compromised root IAM user access key to access the AWS environment via the AWS Golang SDK from a cloud hosting provider IP (ASN AS135629).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The root access key was used to access the AWS environment from a cloud hosting environment using the AWS Golang SDK

attack_chain.evidence_quote: used it to enumerate the environment

attack_chain.description: Imported attacker-owned SSH key pairs into EC2 via the ImportKeyPair API to enable later SSH access to spun-up instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: EC2 SSH access keys were generated for the root access key from a suspicious source IP address ... create SSH key pairs via the ImportKeyPair API

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Spun up ten c5.4xlarge EC2 instances via the RunInstances API.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: spin up ten (10) c5.4xlarge EC2s to mine Monero ... spin up EC2 instances via the RunInstances API call

attack_chain.description: Created new AWS security groups with an ingress rule allowing SSH from the Internet to the new EC2 instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: created new security groups likely to allow inbound access from the Internet ... an ingress rule was created in the bot automation to allow SSH from the Internet

attack_chain.description: Attacker SSH'd into newly created EC2 instances from the Internet using the imported SSH keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The CoinMiner was likely installed via SSH remote access (as a part of the bot)

evidence_chunks.quote: The CoinMiner was likely installed via SSH remote access (as a part of the bot)
The CoinMiner was likely installed via SSH remote access (as a part of the bot)

attack_chain.description: Executed scripted bot automation to install CoinMiner malware on the EC2 instances.
Compromised EC2 instances established C2/mining connections to monerohash[.]com over TCP 7777.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: EC2 instance communicating with a cryptocurrency server (monerohash[.]com on TCP port 7777)

EC2 instance communicating with a cryptocurrency server (monerohash[.]com on TCP port 7777)

attack_chain.description: Used the ten c5.4xlarge EC2 instances to mine Monero cryptocurrency (cryptojacking).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: spin up ten (10) c5.4xlarge EC2s to mine Monero

evidence_chunks.quote: spin up ten (10) c5.4xlarge EC2s to mine Monero
spin up ten (10) c5.4xlarge EC2s to mine Monero

initial_access_vector: Compromised AWS root IAM user access key (source of compromise unknown); used from a cloud hosting provider IP via the AWS Golang SDK.
persistence_mechanism: Imported attacker-owned SSH key pairs (ImportKeyPair) and created security groups allowing inbound SSH, enabling redundant access to newly launched EC2 instances. Continued use of valid root access key.
privilege_escalation: Not applicable — attacker already held root AWS account credentials.
